sections.title: Talking to Sigmund again after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: I asked you to talk to people around Lumbridge to see if any of them know what happened in the cellar.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests for me?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: There was recently some damage to the castle cellar. Part of the wall has collapsed.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: The Duke insists that it was an earthquake, but I think some kind of monsters are to blame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: You should ask people around the town about it. Maybe someone saw something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I'm not interested.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened in the cellar?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: The wall collapsed because of an earthquake. I'm waiting for a team of builders to come and repair it.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: How is the adventuring going, my friend?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh no, it's terrible, isn't it? There was rock dust everywhere, it got on all my ingredients!

lines.speaker: Hans
lines.text: Hello. What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened in the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Hans
lines.text: The wall collapsed. I had to spend all day clearing it up! The Duke says it was an earthquake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened in the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I told you about the creature I saw. You should go and tell the Duke.

sections.title: Talking to Sigmund after talking to Bob

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob says he saw something in the cellar. Like a goblin with big eyes.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: A goblin? This validates my suspicion! We should tell the Duke!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what happened in the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Last night I was in the kitchen and I heard a noise from the cellar. I opened the trapdoor and saw a creature dart into a hole in the wall.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It looked a bit like a goblin, but it had big bulging eyes. It wasn't wearing armour, but it had this odd helmet with a light on it.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: The tunnel was too dark for me to follow it, so I went to tell the Duke. But when we went down to the cellar the hole had been blocked up, and no one believes me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I believe you.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! If you can convince the Duke I'm telling the truth then we can get to the bottom of this mystery.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: You were helping to investigate what happened in the cellar. Come back when you've found something out.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: The Duke asked you to investigate the damage in the cellar. I hope you can find the monsters that are behind this!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob says he saw something in the cellar. Like a goblin with big eyes.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Yes, he mentioned that to me. But I think he was imagining things. Goblins live in natural caves but everyone knows they don't have the wit to make their own tunnels.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Yes your grace, but if there is any possibility that this is a goblin incursion then we should take that possibility very seriously!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think we should at least investigate.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Your grace, this is the adventurer who defeated the dragon Elvarg. I think you should listen to [him/her/them].

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Your grace, this adventurer took that talisman you discovered to Sedridor. I think you should listen to [him/her/them].

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Hmm, very well. I give you permission to investigate this mystery. If there is a blocked tunnel then perhaps you should try to un-block it.

sections.title: Talking to the Duke after opening the tunnel

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I dug through the rubble in the cellar and found a tunnel!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: You see, your grace? It is a goblin attack!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: That tunnel could have many origins. I suggest you investigate further.

sections.title: Talking to Sigmund after opening the tunnel

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I dug through the rubble in the cellar and found a tunnel!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: See, it was goblins! You should tell the Duke.

sections.title: Showing the brooch to the Duke

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I dug through the rubble in the cellar and found a tunnel!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On the ground I found this brooch.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I've never seen anything like that before. It doesn't come from Lumbridge. What do you think, Sigmund?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: It is unknown to me, your grace. But the fact it is there is enough to prove Bob's story. It must have been dropped by a goblin as it fled.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I've never heard of a goblin wearing something so well-crafted.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Then it must have been stolen!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: But it wasn't stolen from us. Where could it be from?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: That doesn't matter! You said yourself that goblins couldn't have made that, so they must have stolen it from somewhere.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Horrible, thieving goblins have broken into our cellar! We must retaliate immediately!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: First we should wipe out the goblins east of the river, then we can march on the goblin village to the north-west...

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I will not commit troops until I have proof that goblins are behind this.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: [Player name], please find out what you can about this brooch. The librarian in Varrock might be able to help identify the symbol.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Hello there, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about this brooch?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I've never seen that symbol before. Where did you find it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In a cave beneath Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Very odd. Have you any idea how it got there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A goblin might have dropped it.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I've never heard of a goblin carrying a brooch like this. But just a minute...

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The other day I filed a book about ancient goblin tribes. It's somewhere at the west end of the library, I think. Maybe that will be of some use.

sections.title: A History of the Goblin Race

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out about the symbol. It was the symbol of one of the ancient goblin tribes!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: You see, your grace? I told you goblins were behind this!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I still find it surprising that such a well-crafted brooch would have a goblin symbol on it. Perhaps you should find out more about this ancient tribe.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out about the symbol. It was the symbol of one of the ancient goblin tribes!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: At last, proof that goblins are behind this! You should tell the Duke at once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'A History of the Goblin Race'. This must be it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey... The symbol of the 'Dorgeshuun' tribe looks just like the symbol on the brooch I found.

sections.title: Speaking to Duke Horacio after the Goblin generals

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I spoke to the goblin generals in the goblin village. They told me about an ancient goblin tribe that went to live underground.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: What more proof do we need? Nasty, smelly goblins have been living under our feet all this time! We must crush them at once!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Hmm, perhaps you are right. I will send word to the army to prepare for an underground assault.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: [Player name], I would still like you to find out more about this tribe. It cannot hurt to know one's enemy.

sections.title: Speaking to Sigmund after the Goblin generals

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I spoke to the goblin generals in the Goblin Village. They told me about an ancient goblin tribe that went to live underground.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: I don't see how there can be any more doubt. You should tell the Duke at once!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever heard of the Dorgeshuun?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Dorgeshuun? That old goblin legend. Lost tribe.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: In time of much-war there many tribes. Big High War-God send Dorgesh tribe to fight beardy-short-people in mountains.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: No no, he send them to fight tall people with biting blades, that what I hear.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: It was beardy-short-people, that how legend go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Make up your minds!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: It was tall people! That what old storyteller say!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No, beardy-short-people. That how legend always go, stupid.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: You stupid!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want me to decide for you again?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That most stupid idea yet. If it happen it either happen one way or the other. You can't just decide which.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Yeah even goblins know that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It doesn't really matter. What happened to them?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Well, they say, 'We not want to fight,' so god punish them.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That silly. No goblin ever say that.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: What happen then?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: They lose battle, that why god punish them.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Ha ha! If they lost battle they all be dead!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: No no, they losing so they run away, and Big High War-God punish them for running away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who were they fighting again?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: It was beardy-short-people in mountains.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: No no, it was tall people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of god would punish them for refusing to fight?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Shh! Must not question Big High War-God!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No, because he much bigger than us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well either way they refused to fight.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Yeah I suppose so.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Anyway then Big High War-God punish them. He turn their insides to stone.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Ha ha ha! That silly!

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Not as silly as green armour! What happen then?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: He put them inside stone! Big hole open in ground and they all go into cave.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That not punishment! Caves nice and cool.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: But then he close cave so they not get out! You not want to stay in cave all the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why were there punished again?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: It because they run away from fight.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: No, it because they refuse to fight night before battle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do goblins make their own tunnels?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: What? Make our own tunnels?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: We hadn't thought of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I found a brooch underground, and I looked up the symbol and it was the symbol of the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: That not look like goblin brooch.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Goblins not wear jewellery.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Well if it Dorgeshuun tribe they not know who won big wars. You should greet them with goblin victory dance!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: No no, you greet them with goblin tribal bow.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Doing bow make you look like a wimp.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: It how tribes greet each other in old days.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Only if they wimpy tribes! Goblin salute strong!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why is the Dorgeshuun tribe underground?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: They punished by Big High War-God.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, you say YOU won the big wars?

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Well we must have done! We goblins! Goblins always win!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Actually we not know who won.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: It was goblins I say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well why not show me both greetings?

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: That good idea. Watch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Bye then.

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Who...who are you? How did you get in here?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Help! A surface-dweller this deep in our mines? We will all be destroyed!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: A human knows ancient greeting?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Perhaps you are friend after all!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Greetings, friend. I am sorry I panicked when I saw you.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Our legends tell of the surface as a place of horror and violence, where the gods forced us to fight in terrible battles.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: When I saw a surface-dweller appear I was afraid it was a return to the old days!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you break in to the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: It was an accident. We were following a seam of iron and suddenly we found ourselves in a room!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: We blocked up our tunnel behind us and ran back here. Then we did what cave goblins always do when there is a problem: we hid and hoped it would go away.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: We meant no harm! Please tell the ruler of the above- people that we want to make peace.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Eeek! The war-dance!

sections.title: Talking to Mistag after first contact

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hello, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: May I mine the rocks here?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: No offence, but we will not let you mine here until there is peace between the humans and the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you show me the way out of the mines?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Certainly. Please hurry, you must avert the war!

sections.title: Using Mistag's brooch on him before the peace treaty

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your brooch?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: We have more important things to think about than a brooch right now. We must prevent a war!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made contact with the cave goblins. They say they were following a seam and broke into the cellar by mistake.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: And I suppose you believe them, goblin-lover?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, they seemed friendlier than most goblins, and nothing was taken from the cellar.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Actually, something was taken. Sigmund has informed me that some of the castle silverware is missing from the cellar.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Unless it is returned, I am afraid I will have no option but war.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: We are preparing to go to war with the underground goblins over the missing silverware. If you think the cave goblins are innocent you should present evidence quickly!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the silverware that was stolen.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: The priceless Lumbridge Silverware! It comes out only on special occasions. It was kept in storage in the cellar, but now the goblins have stolen it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why did no one notice earlier?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: That doesn't matter. You'll never find it, because the goblins took it. So you can't stop the war!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hello, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some silverware has gone missing from the castle, and the Duke thinks you stole it!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Oh no! We would never do such a thing!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: I assure you we took nothing from the cellar. You must convince the Duke we are innocent!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the silverware you stole from the castle cellar?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: What? We didn't take anything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You expect me to believe that? Perhaps Sigmund was right: you're just thieving little goblins!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Why would we want to steal silver? As you see we have much silver already in our mines!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, the Duke says he will attack you unless the silverware is returned.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: The last thing we want is war! If we had anything of yours, we would return it at once. But we don't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sigmund must be a member of the Humans Against Monsters cult!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a H.A.M member, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Well, so what if I am? It's not a crime.

sections.title: Telling the Duke about Sigmund's H.A.M.-loving ways

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know Sigmund is a member of HAM?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Hmm, I had suspected it. But however much I disapprove of the HAM movement it's not actually illegal. I'll keep an eye on him though.

sections.title: Talking to Sigmund before returning the silverware

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the silverware that was stolen.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: The priceless Lumbridge Silverware! It comes out only on special occasions. It was kept in storage in the cellar, but now the goblins have stolen it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why did no one notice earlier?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: That doesn't matter. You'll never find it, because the goblins took it. So you can't stop the war!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I have found it! You put it in the H.A.M. cave!

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: I... I... You're lying! I don't know what you're talking about.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I doing again?

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: You were taking the peace treaty to the cave goblins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I, er, lost the treaty.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: *sigh* All right, here's another copy.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Greetings. Welcome to my castle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the missing silverware in the HAM cave!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Sigmund! Is this your doing?

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Of...of course not! The goblins must have, um, dropped the silverware as they ran away.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Don't lie to me! I knew you were a HAM member but I didn't think you would stoop to this. You are dismissed from my service.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: But don't you see it was for the best? For goblins to be living under our feet like this... ugh. It doesn't matter how civilized they are: all sub-human species must be wiped out!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: That's enough! Get out of my castle now!

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I see I was ill-advised. Unless there is an act of aggression by the cave goblins there is no need for war.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: This peace treaty specifies the border between Lumbridge and the Cave Goblin realm. Please take it to the cave goblins and tell them I would like to meet with their leader to sign it.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hello, friend?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a peace treaty from the Duke of Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: A peace treaty? Then you will not invade?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. As long as you stick to the terms of this treaty there will be no conflict. The Duke of Lumbridge wants to meet your ruler to sign it.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: I will summon Ur-tag, our headman, at once.

sections.title: Cut scene: A meeting of leaders

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your grace, I present Ur-tag, headman of the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: Welcome, Ur-tag. I am sorry that your race came under suspicion.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: I assure you that the warmongering element has been dealt with.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: I apologize for the damage to your cellar. I will send workers to repair the hole.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: No, let it stay. It can be a route of commerce between our lands.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: [Player name], Lumbridge is in your debt. Please accept this ring as a token of my thanks.

lines.speaker: Duke Horacio
lines.text: It is enchanted to save you in your hour of need.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: I too thank you. Accept the freedom of the Dorgeshuun mines.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: These are strange times. I never dreamed that I would see the surface, still less that I would be on friendly terms with its people.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Prattle on, goblin.

lines.speaker: Sigmund
lines.text: Soon you will be destroyed!
